| Context manager       | Scoped enter/exit protocol                            | `with open(...)`          | Accidental exception suppression.               |
| Coroutine             | Awaitable async execution object                      | `async def f()`           | Calling without awaiting.                       |
| Import                | Runtime module initialization and name binding        | `import module`           | Import-time side effects.                       |
| Exception propagation | Non-local failure flow                                | `raise ValueError`        | Catching too early or too broadly.              |
| Exception chaining    | Preserve lower-level cause                            | `raise X from error`      | Losing root cause.                              |

### A.12 Attribute and Descriptor Reference Table

| Mechanism          | Example                         | Meaning                                   | Use carefully because                                     |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| Instance attribute | `self.name = name`              | Per-instance state.                       | May be absent if not initialized.                         |
| Class attribute    | `role = "reader"`               | Shared class-level value/default.         | Mutable values are shared across instances.               |
| Method             | `def method(self):`             | Function descriptor bound to instance.    | `self` is supplied by binding.                            |
| `staticmethod`     | `@staticmethod`                 | Function stored on class without binding. | Often indicates utility-class smell.                      |
| `classmethod`      | `@classmethod`                  | Method bound to class, not instance.      | Good for alternative constructors, not arbitrary globals. |
| `property`         | `@property`                     | Managed attribute access.                 | Should be cheap and unsurprising.                         |
| Descriptor         | `__get__`, `__set__`            | Reusable attribute behavior.              | Powerful but easy to obscure.                             |
| `__getattr__`      | fallback for missing attributes | Proxy/lazy behavior.                      | Weakens static reasoning.                                 |
| `__getattribute__` | intercept every lookup          | Advanced proxy/framework.                 | Easy to break object behavior.                            |
| `__slots__`        | restrict instance attributes    | Memory/control for many objects.          | Reduces dynamic flexibility.                              |

### A.13 Dunder Protocol Reference Table

| Protocol           | Methods                                        | Enables                       | Good use                        | Pitfall                            |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| Representation     | `__repr__`, `__str__`                          | `repr(x)`, `str(x)`, printing | Debug/user display              | Leaking secrets in representation. |
| Truthiness         | `__bool__`, `__len__`                          | `if x:`                       | Domain-specific emptiness/truth | Ambiguous truth behavior.          |
| Size               | `__len__`                                      | `len(x)`                      | Container-like objects          | Returning non-int or negative.     |
| Iteration          | `__iter__`, `__next__`                         | `for`, `iter`, `next`         | Custom iterable/iterator        | Confusing iterable and iterator.   |
| Indexing           | `__getitem__`, `__setitem__`                   | `x[i]`, `x[k]`                | Sequence/mapping objects        | Unclear key/index semantics.       |
| Membership         | `__contains__`                                 | `x in y`                      | Containers                      | Expensive hidden checks.           |
| Callability        | `__call__`                                     | `obj()`                       | Configured function-like object | Hiding stateful behavior.          |
| Context management | `__enter__`, `__exit__`                        | `with`                        | Resources/effects               | Accidental suppression.            |
| Async context      | `__aenter__`, `__aexit__`                      | `async with`                  | Async resources                 | Cleanup/cancellation complexity.   |
| Async iteration    | `__aiter__`, `__anext__`                       | `async for`                   | Async streams                   | Not ordinary iterable.             |
| Equality           | `__eq__`                                       | `==`                          | Value objects                   | Hash contract.                     |
| Hashing            | `__hash__`                                     | dict keys, set members        | Immutable value objects         | Mutable hash fields.               |
| Ordering           | `__lt__`, etc.                                 | sorting/comparison            | Naturally ordered domain        | Arbitrary ordering.                |
| Arithmetic         | `__add__`, etc.                                | operators                     | Numeric/vector/symbolic types   | Surprising side effects.           |
| Attribute access   | `__getattr__`, `__getattribute__`, descriptors | `obj.attr`                    | Framework/proxy behavior        | Hidden dynamic contracts.          |

### A.14 Pattern Matching Reference Table

| Pattern       | Example                 | Meaning                            | Pitfall                                    |                                 |
| ------------- | ----------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------- |
| Wildcard      | `case _:`               | Match anything without binding.    | Placing before more specific cases.        |                                 |
| Capture       | `case name:`            | Match anything and bind to `name`. | Thinking it compares to existing variable. |                                 |
| Literal       | `case 0:`               | Match literal value.               | Overusing for complex conditions.          |                                 |
| OR pattern    | `case "y"               | "yes":`                            | Match alternatives.                        | Bound names must be consistent. |
| Sequence      | `case [x, y]:`          | Match two-element sequence.        | Not list construction.                     |                                 |
| Star sequence | `case [head, *tail]:`   | Match variable-length sequence.    | Materialization/complexity.                |                                 |
| Mapping       | `case {"id": user_id}:` | Match mapping with required key.   | Extra keys are allowed.                    |                                 |
| Class         | `case Point(x, y):`     | Match class and attributes.        | Depends on class pattern support.          |                                 |
| Guard         | `case x if x > 0:`      | Extra condition after match.       | Guard is not part of structural match.     |                                 |
| As pattern    | `case [x, y] as pair:`  | Bind whole matched object too.     | Dense patterns.                            |                                 |

**Rule:** Use `match` for structural alternatives. Use `if` / `elif` for ordinary boolean logic. Use dispatch dictionaries for simple command-to-function routing.

### A.15 Async and Concurrency Reference Table

| Need                                 | Prefer                                      | Why                                   | Avoid                                             |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| Simple sequential task               | Sequential code                             | Lowest complexity.                    | Concurrency without bottleneck.                   |
| Many blocking I/O operations         | `ThreadPoolExecutor` or threads             | Can overlap waiting.                  | Shared mutable state without locks.               |
| Many async-compatible I/O operations | `asyncio`                                   | Cooperative event-loop concurrency.   | Blocking calls inside event loop.                 |
| CPU-bound pure Python                | `ProcessPoolExecutor` / native / vectorized | Real parallelism or native speed.     | Threads for CPU speed under ordinary constraints. |
| Long-lived async service             | Async framework/task ownership              | Structured scheduling.                | Orphan tasks.                                     |
| Shared state among threads           | `threading.Lock`, `queue.Queue`             | Synchronization.                      | Assuming GIL protects invariants.                 |
| Isolated parallel jobs               | Processes                                   | Separate memory and failure boundary. | Sending huge objects repeatedly.                  |
| Background durable work              | Task queue / process model                  | Retry and persistence policy.         | Fire-and-forget local task.                       |
| Async resource                       | `async with`                                | Proper async cleanup.                 | Unclosed clients/sessions.                        |
| Timeout                              | timeout API / future timeout                | Bound waiting.                        | Infinite waits.                                   |
| Cancellation                         | propagate after cleanup                     | Cooperative interruption.             | Swallowing cancellation as normal error.          |

### A.16 Resource Management Reference Table

| Resource/effect        | Correct pattern                                      | Example                             | Pitfall                                   |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| File                   | `with path.open(...) as f:`                          | scoped file access                  | Relying on garbage collection.            |
| Temporary directory    | `with TemporaryDirectory()`                          | scoped temp workspace               | Manual cleanup forgotten.                 |
| Lock                   | `with lock:`                                         | scoped critical section             | Holding lock during slow I/O.             |
| Database transaction   | transaction context                                  | commit/rollback boundary            | Assuming business rollback automatically. |
| Network client/session | context manager or explicit close                    | lifecycle ownership                 | Creating unbounded clients.               |
| Subprocess             | `subprocess.run([...], check=True)`                  | checked external command            | Ignoring return code.                     |
| Environment variables  | parse into config once                               | config boundary                     | Reading throughout core logic.            |
| Time                   | inject `now` or use clock abstraction for core logic | testability                         | Hard-coded current time.                  |
| Randomness             | inject generator; use `secrets` for security         | deterministic tests / secure tokens | `random` for secrets.                     |
| Logging                | module logger                                        | diagnostics                         | Logging secrets.                          |
| Cache                  | bounded and invalidated                              | performance                         | Unbounded memory growth.                  |
| Async resource         | `async with`                                         | async cleanup                       | Sync close in async lifecycle.            |

### A.17 Security Reference Table

| Risk                   | Dangerous pattern            | Safer pattern                              |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| SQL injection          | `f"SELECT ... {value}"`      | Parameterized query.                       |
| Shell injection        | `shell=True` with user input | Argument list, avoid shell.                |
| Unsafe deserialization | `pickle.loads(untrusted)`    | JSON/TOML plus validation.                 |
| Unsafe YAML            | object-construction loader   | Safe loader and schema validation.         |
| Path traversal         | `base / user_path` blindly   | Resolve and constrain under base.          |
| Secret leakage         | Logging tokens/env dumps     | Redact or omit secrets.                    |
| Dynamic code           | `eval(user_input)`           | Explicit parser or whitelist.              |
| Dynamic import         | Import arbitrary user string | Registry or whitelist.                     |
| Weak token randomness  | `random`                     | `secrets`.                                 |
| Raw traceback exposure | Show traceback to user       | Sanitized message; internal logs.          |
| Dependency risk        | Blind upgrades               | Review, audit, lock apps.                  |
| Plugin trust           | Load arbitrary plugin        | Protocol, capability, sandbox/trust model. |

### A.18 Testing Reference Table

| Code shape             | Best test approach                    | Example focus                              |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| Pure function          | Direct unit tests                     | Input/output and edge cases.               |
| Parser/validator       | Boundary tests                        | Bad types, missing keys, invalid values.   |
| Dataclass/value object | Invariant tests                       | Invalid construction.                      |
| File adapter           | Temporary files/directories           | Missing file, encoding, write behavior.    |
| Database adapter       | Test database/integration test        | Transactions, not-found, constraints.      |
| Network client         | Fake server/mock boundary/integration | Timeout, status, malformed response.       |
| CLI                    | Call `main(argv)`                     | Exit codes, stdout/stderr.                 |
| Config loader          | Fake environment mapping              | Missing/invalid env values.                |
| Time-dependent logic   | Inject `now`                          | Expiration boundaries.                     |
| Random logic           | Inject RNG/seed                       | Reproducible behavior.                     |
| Async function         | Async-aware test runner               | Awaiting, cancellation, task ownership.    |
| Legacy code            | Characterization tests                | Preserve current behavior before refactor. |
| Bug fix                | Regression test                       | Prevent recurrence.                        |
| Algorithm              | Property-based tests if appropriate   | Invariants over generated cases.           |

### A.19 Performance Reference Table

| Symptom              | Likely cause                      | First check               | Common fix                                    |
| -------------------- | --------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| Slow repeated lookup | Linear scans                      | Profile / inspect loops   | Use `dict` or `set`.                          |
| Slow queue           | `list.pop(0)`                     | Inspect queue operations  | Use `deque`.                                  |
| Slow numeric loop    | Python per-element work           | Profile hot loop          | Vectorize/native/process.                     |
| Slow startup         | Heavy imports/import-time effects | Import timing             | Lazy optional imports; reduce top-level work. |
| Slow API request     | DB/network/serialization          | Trace boundary timing     | Batch, index, timeout, cache carefully.       |
| High memory          | Large lists/caches/tracebacks     | Memory profiling          | Stream, bound cache, release references.      |
| Slow JSON/CSV        | Serialization/parsing cost        | Profile                   | Batch, faster library if justified.           |
| Slow async system    | Blocking event loop               | Find sync calls           | Use async-compatible API or executor.         |
| Slow process pool    | Serialization overhead            | Measure payload/task size | Batch tasks, reduce data transfer.            |
| Flaky performance    | External service variability      | Add observability         | Timeouts, retries, caching, queueing.         |
| Slow tests           | Real I/O/network                  | Test profiling            | Fakes, fixtures, split integration tests.     |

### A.20 Packaging and Environment Reference Table

| Need                        | Use                          | Notes                                                   |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| Isolated environment        | `python -m venv .venv`       | Baseline standard-library option.                       |
| Project metadata            | `pyproject.toml`             | Central modern metadata/config file.                    |
| Library dependency ranges   | `[project].dependencies`     | Avoid overpinning if publishing library.                |
| Application reproducibility | lock/pinned workflow         | Tooling varies; principle is exact deploy environment.  |
| Build backend               | `[build-system]`             | Declares build requirements/backend.                    |
| Package data                | `importlib.resources`        | Avoid current-working-directory assumptions.            |
| Installed metadata          | `importlib.metadata`         | Query installed packages.                               |
| CLI entry point             | project scripts/entry points | Better than asking users to run files directly.         |
| Python version support      | `requires-python` and CI     | Syntax must match declared support.                     |
| Tool config                 | `[tool.*]` sections          | Keep formatter/linter/type checker config discoverable. |
| Release artifact            | wheel/sdist                  | Test built artifact, not only source tree.              |
| Dependency updates          | review/test/audit            | Treat as code changes.                                  |

### A.21 API Compatibility Reference Table

| Change                            | Compatibility risk                | Safer approach                            |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| Rename public function            | High                              | Re-export old name with deprecation.      |
| Move public module                | High                              | Preserve import path temporarily.         |
| Add keyword-only option           | Usually low                       | Prefer keyword-only for future expansion. |
| Add positional parameter          | Medium/high                       | Avoid; use keyword-only default.          |
| Reorder parameters                | High                              | Do not do in public API.                  |
| Change return `list` to generator | High                              | Document or provide new function.         |
| Change exception class            | Medium/high                       | Subclass old exception or deprecate.      |
| Change serialized key             | High                              | Version schema or support both.           |
| Tighten accepted input            | Medium                            | Document and test migration.              |
| Drop Python version               | High for affected users           | Announce clearly.                         |
| Change mutability of return       | Medium/high                       | Preserve ownership contract.              |
| Remove deprecated feature         | Expected if deprecation was clear | Provide migration path first.             |

### A.22 Code Review Checklist Table

| Area              | Review question                                                         |
| ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Purpose           | Does the function/module have one clear responsibility?                 |
| Data model        | Are domain concepts represented clearly?                                |
| Trust boundary    | Is external data parsed and validated?                                  |
| Type hints        | Do annotations express real contracts?                                  |
| Errors            | Are failures specific and handled at recovery boundaries?               |
| Mutability        | Is ownership of mutable data clear?                                     |
| Effects           | Are I/O, network, DB, time, env, random, logging visible?               |
| Resources         | Are resources scoped with context managers or explicit lifecycle?       |
| Imports           | Any circular imports or import-time side effects?                       |
| Security          | Any unsafe shell, SQL, pickle, path, secret, or dynamic execution risk? |
| Concurrency       | Is task/resource ownership clear? Any blocking async calls?             |
| Tests             | Are success, failure, and boundary cases tested?                        |
| Performance       | Any obvious algorithmic or memory issue?                                |
| API compatibility | Does this change public behavior?                                       |
| Readability       | Is this understandable under maintenance pressure?                      |

### A.23 Anti-Pattern Quick Reference Table

| Anti-pattern             | Typical symptom                               | Correction                                         |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Mutable default          | `def f(xs=[]):`                               | Use `None` or sentinel; create inside function.    |
| Bare `except`            | `except:`                                     | Catch specific exception.                          |
| Raw dict leakage         | `data["x"]` everywhere                        | Parse into model at boundary.                      |
| Primitive obsession      | all IDs/money/states as `str`/`int`           | Use value objects, `NewType`, `Enum` where useful. |
| Utility class            | only `@staticmethod`s                         | Use module-level functions.                        |
| God object               | `Manager` controls everything                 | Split responsibilities.                            |
| Positional boolean       | `f(x, True)`                                  | Keyword-only flag.                                 |
| Hidden I/O               | property/constructor does network/file access | Use explicit method or adapter.                    |
| Global config reads      | `os.environ` in core logic                    | Parse config once and pass object.                 |
| Overmocking              | tests assert every helper call                | Test behavior; fake boundaries.                    |
| Async blocking           | `time.sleep()` in `async def`                 | Use `await asyncio.sleep()` or executor.           |
| Reflection dispatch      | `getattr(obj, user_input)`                    | Use explicit registry/whitelist.                   |
| Untrusted pickle         | `pickle.loads(payload)`                       | Use safe format and validation.                    |
| Shell injection          | `shell=True` with input                       | Use argument list.                                 |
| `Any` leakage            | typed code becomes unchecked                  | Isolate dynamic boundary.                          |
| Premature abstraction    | class hierarchy before stable concept         | Start with functions/modules/composition.          |
| Overdense comprehension  | complex side effects in expression            | Use explicit loop.                                 |
| Import-time side effects | module connects/starts work on import         | Move to explicit setup/main.                       |
| Unbounded cache          | global dict/lru cache grows forever           | Bound/invalidate.                                  |
| Public internals         | external code imports `_internal`             | Define stable facade.                              |

### A.24 “Which Construct Should I Use?” Quick Table

| Situation                                        | Best first choice              |       |
| ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------ | ----- |
| Need transform one value                         | Function                       |       |
| Need transform collection simply                 | Comprehension                  |       |
| Need multi-step collection logic                 | Explicit loop                  |       |
| Need lazy one-pass stream                        | Generator                      |       |
| Need maintain state over time                    | Class                          |       |
| Need small configured function                   | Closure or `functools.partial` |       |
| Need behavior contract                           | `Protocol`                     |       |
| Need shared implementation with stable hierarchy | Inheritance                    |       |
| Need compose capabilities                        | Composition                    |       |
| Need wrap cross-cutting behavior                 | Decorator                      |       |
| Need resource scope                              | Context manager                |       |
| Need finite states                               | `Enum` / `Literal`             |       |
| Need validated external data                     | Parser / validation model      |       |
| Need passive structured data                     | `dataclass`                    |       |
| Need runtime domain invariant                    | Value object / custom class    |       |
| Need dictionary-shaped static payload            | `TypedDict` plus validation    |       |
| Need unknown safe input                          | `object` plus narrowing        |       |
| Need dynamic temporary escape                    | `Any`, localized               |       |
| Need ordinary absence                            | `T                             | None` |
| Need serious failure                             | Exception                      |       |
| Need batch diagnostics                           | Result object                  |       |
| Need performance improvement                     | Profile first                  |       |

### A.25 Minimal Professional Defaults Table

| Area            | Default                                                                     |                    |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ |
| Paths           | Use `pathlib.Path`.                                                         |                    |
| Files           | Use `with` and explicit encoding for text.                                  |                    |
| JSON            | Decode, then validate.                                                      |                    |
| Config          | Parse once into immutable config model.                                     |                    |
| Errors          | Use specific exceptions and `raise ... from error`.                         |                    |
| Logging         | Use `logging.getLogger(__name__)`.                                          |                    |
| CLI             | Use `argparse` and `main() -> int`.                                         |                    |
| Data records    | Use `dataclass` when structure is stable.                                   |                    |
| Optional values | Use `T                                                                      | None`and`is None`. |
| Collections     | Choose by operation pattern.                                                |                    |
| Type hints      | Type public/core functions meaningfully.                                    |                    |
| Unknown data    | Use `object`, not `Any`, when narrowing is required.                        |                    |
| Resources       | Use context managers.                                                       |                    |
| Dependencies    | Use virtual environment and project metadata.                               |                    |
| Testing         | Test pure logic and boundaries separately.                                  |                    |
| Formatting      | Automate with formatter.                                                    |                    |
| Linting         | Enforce high-value rules in CI.                                             |                    |
| Performance     | Measure before optimizing.                                                  |                    |
| Security        | Avoid shell strings, untrusted pickle, secret logging.                      |                    |
| Public API      | Treat signature, return type, exception type, and import path as contracts. |                    |
## Appendix B — Python Error Diagnosis Table

### B.1 How to Read a Python Error

A Python error usually contains four useful layers:

| Layer           | What it tells you                    | What to inspect                                          |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| Exception type  | General class of failure             | `TypeError`, `ValueError`, `AttributeError`, etc.        |
| Error message   | Local symptom                        | Wrong argument count, missing key, unsupported operation |
| Traceback stack | Execution path to failure            | The last project-code frame before the exception         |
| Context/cause   | Previous exception or explicit chain | `raise ... from error`, nested failures                  |

A traceback should normally be read from bottom upward for the immediate exception, then upward again to find the first project-level cause.

```text
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "main.py", line 20, in <module>
    main()
  File "main.py", line 15, in main
    count = parse_count(raw)
  File "main.py", line 7, in parse_count
    return int(raw)
ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: 'abc'
```

| Part                        | Diagnosis                                 |
| --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `ValueError`                | Type is acceptable, but value is invalid. |
| `invalid literal for int()` | `int()` could not parse the text.         |
| `return int(raw)`           | Immediate failing operation.              |
| `parse_count(raw)`          | Caller passed an invalid value.           |

**Rule:** The last line tells the exception type and local symptom. The traceback frames tell how the program reached it.

### B.2 Core Exception Diagnosis Table

| Exception             | Common symptom                                    | Usual semantic cause                                             | First diagnostic question                                       | Typical fix                                                                           |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `SyntaxError`         | Program fails before running                      | Source code is not grammatically valid                           | Is the code parseable?                                          | Fix punctuation, indentation, quotes, parentheses, invalid syntax for Python version. |
| `IndentationError`    | Unexpected indent / expected block                | Indentation structure invalid                                    | Did a block start or end incorrectly?                           | Align indentation consistently.                                                       |
| `TabError`            | Inconsistent tabs and spaces                      | Mixed indentation styles                                         | Are tabs and spaces mixed?                                      | Use spaces consistently.                                                              |
| `NameError`           | Name is not defined                               | Name lookup failed                                               | Was the name imported, assigned, or spelled correctly?          | Define/import name before use; fix spelling/scope.                                    |
| `UnboundLocalError`   | Local variable referenced before assignment       | Assignment in function made name local                           | Is the function assigning to a name also read from outer scope? | Return values explicitly, use object state, or use `nonlocal`/`global` deliberately.  |
| `TypeError`           | Unsupported operation or wrong call shape         | Object does not support protocol or argument type/count is wrong | What type was actually passed?                                  | Check function signature, conversions, protocol, argument names.                      |
| `ValueError`          | Invalid value                                     | Type is acceptable but value is outside valid domain             | Is parsing or domain validation failing?                        | Validate earlier; raise/handle domain-specific error.                                 |
| `AttributeError`      | Object has no attribute                           | Attribute lookup failed                                          | What is `type(obj)` at failure?                                 | Fix object construction, spelling, protocol assumption, or optional `None` case.      |
| `KeyError`            | Missing mapping key                               | Dictionary/mapping lookup failed                                 | Is the key guaranteed by schema?                                | Validate input, use `.get()` when absence is normal, or handle missing key.           |
| `IndexError`          | List index out of range                           | Sequence index outside bounds                                    | Is the sequence length as expected?                             | Fix loop bounds, check length, avoid manual indexing.                                 |
| `StopIteration`       | Iterator exhausted                                | `next()` called after no more values                             | Is exhaustion normal?                                           | Handle with default, loop normally, or avoid reusing exhausted iterator.              |
| `RuntimeError`        | Generic runtime failure                           | State or runtime condition invalid                               | Is there a more specific cause?                                 | Use specific exception where designing API; inspect message.                          |
| `AssertionError`      | Assertion failed                                  | Internal invariant or test expectation failed                    | Is this user input or internal invariant?                       | Do not use `assert` for external validation; fix invariant/test.                      |
| `ImportError`         | Import failed after module found or during import | Import target unavailable or circular import                     | Is this environment, name, or circular import?                  | Check installed package, import path, module names, dependency direction.             |
| `ModuleNotFoundError` | No module named X                                 | Import system cannot locate module                               | Is the package installed in this interpreter?                   | Activate correct environment; install dependency; fix import name.                    |
| `FileNotFoundError`   | No such file or directory                         | Path does not exist at use time                                  | Is the path relative, configured, or generated?                 | Use `Path`, check working directory/config, handle absence.                           |
| `PermissionError`     | Permission denied                                 | OS denied file/process/socket operation                          | Which user/process owns resource?                               | Fix permissions, path, sandbox, or run context.                                       |
| `TimeoutError`        | Operation timed out                               | External operation exceeded time bound                           | What was waiting?                                               | Add retry/backoff, timeout policy, observability.                                     |
| `OSError`             | OS-level failure                                  | Filesystem/process/socket/environment issue                      | Which OS call failed?                                           | Inspect `errno`, path, permissions, platform behavior.                                |
| `MemoryError`         | Out of memory                                     | Program attempted excessive allocation                           | What is being materialized?                                     | Stream, batch, reduce object graph, avoid huge lists.                                 |
| `RecursionError`      | Maximum recursion depth exceeded                  | Recursion too deep or no base case                               | Is recursion bounded?                                           | Add base case, use explicit stack, validate nested input.                             |

### B.3 Syntax and Parsing Errors

| Error message                                   | Likely cause                                           | Example                         | Fix                                                     |
| ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `SyntaxError: invalid syntax`                   | Invalid grammar, often near previous token             | `if x print(x)`                 | Add missing colon/block structure.                      |
| `SyntaxError: expected ':'`                     | Block header missing colon                             | `if x`                          | Write `if x:`                                           |
| `SyntaxError: '(' was never closed`             | Unclosed delimiter                                     | `print((1 + 2)`                 | Close parentheses/brackets/braces.                      |
| `SyntaxError: unterminated string literal`      | Missing closing quote                                  | `"hello`                        | Close quote; check escaping.                            |
| `SyntaxError: invalid character`                | Non-ASCII lookalike punctuation or invisible character | `x＝1`                           | Replace with ASCII operator/punctuation.                |
| `SyntaxError: 'return' outside function`        | `return` used outside `def`                            | top-level `return 1`            | Use `raise SystemExit(...)` or put inside function.     |
| `SyntaxError: 'await' outside function`         | `await` outside async context                          | top-level `await f()` in script | Use `asyncio.run(main())` or async-capable environment. |
| `SyntaxError: name capture ...`                 | Pattern-matching name binding issue                    | invalid `case` structure        | Review `match` pattern rules.                           |
| `IndentationError: expected an indented block`  | Block header not followed by indented suite            | `if ok:\nprint(ok)`             | Indent block body.                                      |
| `IndentationError: unexpected indent`           | Extra indentation without block                        | indented line at top level      | Remove indentation or add block header.                 |
| `TabError: inconsistent use of tabs and spaces` | Mixed indentation                                      | tabs and spaces in same block   | Convert indentation to spaces.                          |

**Diagnosis rule:** Syntax errors often point to the place where the parser became confused, not always where the real mistake began. Check the previous line first when the marked line looks correct.

### B.4 Name, Scope, and Binding Errors

| Error                                       | Example                                           | Semantic cause                                        | Fix                                             |
| ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `NameError: name 'x' is not defined`        | `print(x)` before assignment                      | Name lookup failed in local/enclosing/global/builtins | Assign/import before use.                       |
| `NameError` from typo                       | `pritn(value)`                                    | Misspelled name                                       | Fix spelling; use editor/linter.                |
| `NameError` after conditional assignment    | `if ok: x = 1; print(x)`                          | Branch may not bind name                              | Initialize before branch or handle both paths.  |
| `UnboundLocalError`                         | `count += 1` inside function using global `count` | Assignment makes `count` local                        | Use return value/object state; rarely `global`. |
| `UnboundLocalError` with closure            | inner function assigns outer variable             | Need explicit closure rebinding                       | Use `nonlocal` deliberately.                    |
| `NameError` from import alias               | `import numpy as np; numpy.array(...)`            | Only alias was bound                                  | Use `np` or import full name.                   |
| `NameError` after `from module import name` | using `module.name`                               | Module name not bound                                 | Use imported `name` or `import module`.         |
| Shadowed built-in causes later failure      | `list = [1, 2]; list("abc")`                      | Built-in `list` name rebound                          | Rename variable.                                |

Example:

```python
count = 0

def increment() -> int:
    count += 1
    return count
```

This fails because `count += 1` is both read and assignment. Python treats `count` as local inside the function.

Better:

```python
def increment(count: int) -> int:
    return count + 1
```

or use stateful object if shared state is intended.

### B.5 Type Errors

| Error message pattern                                   | Likely cause                               | Example                                      | Fix                                                   |
| ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `unsupported operand type(s)`                           | Operator not supported by operand types    | `"3" + 4`                                    | Convert explicitly or fix data source.                |
| `can only concatenate str ...`                          | Mixing string with non-string              | `"id=" + 1`                                  | Use f-string or convert: `f"id={id_}"`.               |
| `'NoneType' object is not ...`                          | Optional value used as real object         | `user.name` when `user is None`              | Check `None`; use required lookup if absence invalid. |
| `object is not callable`                                | Calling non-callable object                | `items()` when `items` is list               | Remove call or fix variable shadowing.                |
| `missing required positional argument`                  | Function called with too few args          | `f(1)` but `f(a,b)`                          | Pass required args or defaults.                       |
| `got an unexpected keyword argument`                    | Wrong keyword name or API version          | `f(timeout=1)` unsupported                   | Check signature/version.                              |
| `takes N positional arguments but M were given`         | Wrong call shape; method binding issue     | `obj.method(a,b)`                            | Check `self`, staticmethod/classmethod, signature.    |
| `argument of type X is not iterable`                    | Used `in` on non-container                 | `"a" in None`                                | Check optional value or use correct container.        |
| `unhashable type: 'list'`                               | Mutable object used as dict key/set member | `{[1,2]: "x"}`                               | Use tuple/frozenset or stable key.                    |
| `list indices must be integers or slices, not str`      | Treating list like dict                    | `items["id"]`                                | Inspect JSON shape; index list or parse objects.      |
| `string indices must be integers`                       | Treating string like dict/list of dict     | `item["id"]` when `item` is string           | Validate decoded data shape.                          |
| `a bytes-like object is required, not 'str'`            | Text used where bytes expected             | writing str to binary file                   | Encode text or open text mode.                        |
| `can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes` | Mixed datetime timezone awareness          | comparing `datetime.now()` with UTC datetime | Use timezone-aware datetimes consistently.            |

**Diagnosis rule:** For `TypeError`, inspect the actual runtime type with `type(value)` near the failing line. Static expectations are often wrong at external-data boundaries.

### B.6 Value Errors and Domain Validation

| Error message                         | Likely cause                              | Example                     | Fix                                                 |
| ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `invalid literal for int()`           | Text cannot parse as integer              | `int("abc")`                | Validate string; handle parse failure.              |
| `could not convert string to float`   | Text cannot parse as float                | `float("1,2")`              | Normalize input or locale-specific parsing.         |
| `too many values to unpack`           | Iterable has more values than targets     | `a, b = [1,2,3]`            | Match target count or use starred unpacking.        |
| `not enough values to unpack`         | Iterable has fewer values than targets    | `a, b = [1]`                | Validate length or adjust unpacking.                |
| `max() arg is an empty sequence`      | Aggregate called on empty input           | `max([])`                   | Provide default or check emptiness.                 |
| `min() arg is an empty sequence`      | Same for min                              | `min([])`                   | Provide default or check.                           |
| `math domain error`                   | Numeric input outside mathematical domain | `math.sqrt(-1)`             | Validate domain or use complex math.                |
| `time data ... does not match format` | Date/time string format mismatch          | `strptime` wrong format     | Match exact format or use ISO parser if applicable. |
| `bad marshal data` / parse failure    | Corrupted or incompatible binary data     | loading invalid cached data | Clear cache or validate file.                       |

**Design rule:** `ValueError` often indicates missing validation at a boundary. If the invalid value comes from user input, config, CLI, JSON, CSV, database, or environment, add a parser/validator before core logic.

### B.7 Attribute Errors

| Symptom                                  | Likely cause                         | Diagnostic question                       | Fix                                                |
| ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `'NoneType' object has no attribute 'x'` | Optional lookup returned `None`      | Which function may return `None`?         | Check `None` or use required-return function.      |
| `'dict' object has no attribute 'x'`     | Treating dict like object            | Is this raw JSON/dict?                    | Use `data["x"]`, parse into dataclass, or adapter. |
| `'list' object has no attribute 'x'`     | Treating list as single object       | Did API return multiple items?            | Iterate or select one item.                        |
| Custom object lacks attribute            | Not initialized or wrong class       | Was `__init__` called and field assigned? | Initialize consistently; use dataclass.            |
| Property raises `AttributeError`         | Descriptor/property internal failure | Is the property doing lookup?             | Inspect property body.                             |
| Attribute exists sometimes               | Conditional assignment               | Is field assigned in all branches?        | Initialize in constructor.                         |
| Decorated function missing metadata      | Decorator did not use `wraps`        | Is `__name__`/signature changed?          | Use `functools.wraps`.                             |

Example:

```python
user = find_user(user_id)
print(user.email)
```

If `find_user` returns `User | None`, this may fail.

Better:

```python
user = find_user(user_id)
if user is None:
    raise UserNotFoundError(user_id)

print(user.email)
```

### B.8 Key, Index, and Collection Errors

| Error                                                    | Common cause                  | Example                             | Fix                                              |
| -------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `KeyError`                                               | Missing dict key              | `config["timeout"]`                 | Validate config or use `.get()` with default.    |
| `IndexError`                                             | Index outside sequence        | `items[0]` on empty list            | Check emptiness or handle absence.               |
| `TypeError: unhashable type`                             | Mutable key in dict/set       | `set([[1]])`                        | Convert to immutable representation.             |
| `RuntimeError: dictionary changed size during iteration` | Mutating dict while iterating | deleting keys in `for k in d:`      | Iterate over `list(d)` or collect changes first. |
| `RuntimeError: set changed size during iteration`        | Mutating set during iteration | adding/removing during loop         | Iterate over copy or collect operations.         |
| Unexpected empty generator                               | Reusing exhausted iterator    | `list(g); list(g)`                  | Recreate generator or materialize once.          |
| Duplicate keys silently overwritten                      | Dict construction             | `{user.id: user for user in users}` | Check duplicates explicitly if invalid.          |

Example duplicate-key check:

```python
def index_users(users: list[User]) -> dict[int, User]:
    result: dict[int, User] = {}

    for user in users:
        if user.id in result:
            raise ValueError(f"duplicate user id: {user.id}")
        result[user.id] = user

    return result
```

### B.9 Import and Packaging Errors

| Error                                                                 | Likely cause                                      | Diagnostic question                           | Fix                                                                |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'x'`                            | Package not installed in active interpreter       | Which `python` is running?                    | Activate correct env; install package; check `python -m pip`.      |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'x'`                                 | Name not exported or circular import              | Does module define name at import completion? | Fix name, avoid circular imports, import from correct module.      |
| `ImportError: attempted relative import with no known parent package` | Running package module as script path             | Was file executed directly?                   | Use `python -m package.module`.                                    |
| Circular import partial initialization                                | Modules import each other at top level            | Do two modules depend mutually?               | Move shared definitions, use type-only imports, invert dependency. |
| Local file shadows stdlib/package                                     | File named `json.py`, `typing.py`, etc.           | What is `module.__file__`?                    | Rename local file.                                                 |
| Package works in source tree but not installed                        | Path/resource assumption                          | Does code rely on CWD?                        | Use package resources and test built artifact.                     |
| `pip install` succeeds but import fails                               | Different distribution/import name or environment | Is import name same as package name?          | Check docs; verify interpreter/env.                                |
| Native extension import error                                         | Missing binary dependency or incompatible wheel   | Platform/Python version compatible?           | Install compatible wheel/deps; check Python version.               |

Useful diagnostics:

```bash
python -c "import sys; print(sys.executable)"
python -m pip show package-name
python -c "import module; print(module.__file__)"
```

### B.10 File, Path, and OS Errors

| Error                                    | Common cause                          | Example                               | Fix                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `FileNotFoundError`                      | Wrong path or working directory       | `open("data.txt")`                    | Use absolute/configured `Path`; inspect `Path.cwd()`. |
| `PermissionError`                        | Insufficient permissions              | reading protected file                | Change permissions/path/user.                         |
| `IsADirectoryError`                      | Tried file operation on directory     | `Path("dir").read_text()`             | Check `path.is_file()`.                               |
| `NotADirectoryError`                     | Path component expected directory     | `a/b` where `a` is file               | Validate path components.                             |
| `OSError: [Errno 36] File name too long` | Path/name exceeds OS limit            | generated filename                    | Shorten/sanitize.                                     |
| `UnicodeDecodeError`                     | Wrong text encoding                   | reading bytes as UTF-8                | Specify correct encoding or binary mode.              |
| `UnicodeEncodeError`                     | Cannot encode text to target encoding | writing non-ASCII to limited encoding | Use UTF-8 or correct output encoding.                 |
| `shutil.SameFileError`                   | Copy source and destination same      | `copy2(a, a)`                         | Check paths before copy.                              |
| `TemporaryDirectory` files missing       | Used temp path after context exit     | returning temp path                   | Keep use inside context or manage lifetime.           |

**Path diagnosis rule:** Always distinguish path construction, current working directory, file existence, permission, encoding, and race conditions.

### B.11 Text, Bytes, Unicode, and Regex Errors

| Error/symptom                                                   | Likely cause                          | Example                                        | Fix                                                     |
| --------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `UnicodeDecodeError`                                            | Bytes decoded with wrong encoding     | `path.read_text()` with default/wrong encoding | Pass correct `encoding`.                                |
| `UnicodeEncodeError`                                            | Text cannot encode to output encoding | printing/writing special characters            | Use UTF-8 output or handle encoding.                    |
| `TypeError: cannot use a string pattern on a bytes-like object` | Regex pattern type mismatch           | `re.search("x", b"x")`                         | Use text with text pattern or bytes with bytes pattern. |
| Regex matches too much                                          | Greedy pattern                        | `.*` spans too far                             | Use specific pattern or non-greedy carefully.           |
| Regex validation accepts invalid text                           | Used `search()`                       | `re.search(r"\d+", "abc123x")`                 | Use `fullmatch()` for whole-string validation.          |
| Backslashes behave oddly                                        | Missing raw string                    | `"\bword\b"`                                   | Use `r"\bword\b"`.                                      |
| Case-insensitive comparison fails in edge cases                 | Used `.lower()`                       | Unicode case issue                             | Use `.casefold()` where appropriate.                    |
| Visually same text not equal                                    | Unicode normalization difference      | composed/decomposed forms                      | Use `unicodedata.normalize()` with domain policy.       |

### B.12 JSON, CSV, TOML, and Serialization Errors

| Error/symptom                                          | Likely cause                         | Example                          | Fix                                    |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `json.JSONDecodeError`                                 | Invalid JSON syntax                  | trailing comma, single quotes    | Fix JSON; handle parse error.          |
| `TypeError: Object of type X is not JSON serializable` | Custom object passed to `json.dumps` | dataclass/object                 | Convert to plain dict/list/scalars.    |
| Decoded JSON shape wrong                               | JSON valid but not expected schema   | list instead of dict             | Validate after decoding.               |
| CSV rows split incorrectly                             | Manual `.split(",")`                 | quoted comma                     | Use `csv` module.                      |
| CSV newline issues                                     | Missing `newline=""`                 | blank lines on some platforms    | Open CSV files with `newline=""`.      |
| TOML parse error                                       | Invalid TOML syntax                  | wrong section/quote              | Fix TOML; validate config.             |
| `pickle` security risk                                 | Loading untrusted pickle             | `pickle.loads(payload)`          | Do not load untrusted pickle.          |
| SQLite parameter mismatch                              | Wrong number of placeholders         | SQL has 2 `?`, tuple has 1 value | Match SQL placeholders and parameters. |
| SQL injection risk                                     | f-string SQL                         | `f"... {email}"`                 | Use parameterized query.               |

**Boundary rule:** Decoding is not validation. After `json.loads`, `csv.DictReader`, or `tomllib.loads`, validate shape and domain constraints before internal use.

### B.13 Datetime and Time Errors

| Error/symptom                                                      | Likely cause                       | Example                           | Fix                                                  |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes` | Mixed timezone awareness           | `datetime.now()` vs UTC datetime  | Use aware datetimes consistently.                    |
| Wrong elapsed time                                                 | Used wall-clock time               | `datetime.now()` around operation | Use `time.monotonic()`.                              |
| DST-related surprise                                               | Local time transition              | recurring local schedule          | Use `zoneinfo` and domain-specific scheduling logic. |
| Parsed date wrong                                                  | Format mismatch or ambiguous date  | `01/02/2026`                      | Use explicit format/policy.                          |
| Timezone missing from stored timestamp                             | Stored naive datetime              | DB/API timestamp                  | Store UTC or explicit timezone policy.               |
| Sleep blocks async code                                            | Used `time.sleep()` in `async def` | event loop stalls                 | Use `await asyncio.sleep()`.                         |

### B.14 Function, Decorator, and Callable Errors

| Error/symptom                                             | Likely cause                              | Example                          | Fix                                                            |
| --------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `TypeError: missing required positional argument: 'self'` | Method called on class without instance   | `C.method()`                     | Instantiate or use `@staticmethod`/`@classmethod` if intended. |
| Decorated function loses name/signature                   | Missing `functools.wraps`                 | decorator returns wrapper        | Add `@wraps(function)`.                                        |
| Mutable default state accumulates                         | Default object reused                     | `def f(xs=[]):`                  | Use `None`/sentinel and construct inside.                      |
| Callback receives wrong args                              | Callback contract mismatch                | API calls `callback(x,y)`        | Document/type callback signature.                              |
| `object is not callable`                                  | Variable shadows function or wrong object | `list = []; list()`              | Rename variable; inspect type.                                 |
| Decorator runs too early                                  | Definition-time behavior                  | registration/import side effects | Move runtime work into wrapper or explicit setup.              |
| `functools.partial` confusing error                       | Arguments bound incorrectly               | too many/missing args            | Inspect resulting callable and original signature.             |

### B.15 Class, Dataclass, and OOP Errors

| Error/symptom                            | Likely cause                           | Example                               | Fix                                                      |
| ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| Shared state across instances            | Mutable class attribute                | `items = []` in class body            | Initialize in `__init__` or dataclass `default_factory`. |
| Dataclass mutable default error          | Mutable default field                  | `items: list[int] = []`               | Use `field(default_factory=list)`.                       |
| Property causes slow/failing access      | Property hides operation               | `user.profile` does network call      | Use explicit method.                                     |
| `super()` calls wrong method chain       | MRO misunderstanding                   | multiple inheritance                  | Inspect MRO; design cooperative `super()`.               |
| Attribute cannot be set                  | `__slots__` or property without setter | `obj.x = 1`                           | Add slot/setter or use normal class.                     |
| Hash changes after mutation              | Mutable object used as hashable        | custom `__hash__` over mutable fields | Make immutable or avoid hashing.                         |
| Equality breaks set/dict behavior        | `__eq__` without compatible `__hash__` | value object                          | Use frozen dataclass or define both carefully.           |
| Class variable treated as instance field | Missing `ClassVar` in dataclass        | shared config field                   | Annotate `ClassVar` or use instance field.               |

### B.16 Iterator and Generator Errors

| Symptom                                | Likely cause                         | Example                                      | Fix                                                          |
| -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Second loop produces nothing           | Generator exhausted                  | `for x in g`; then again                     | Recreate generator or materialize list.                      |
| Error occurs later than expected       | Lazy evaluation                      | generator expression                         | Consume near creation if validation timing matters.          |
| File closed during iteration           | Generator/resource lifetime mismatch | returning generator from closed file context | Keep file open during consumption or design context manager. |
| `StopIteration` leaks unexpectedly     | Manual `next()` or generator misuse  | `next(it)` without default                   | Use loop or `next(it, default)`.                             |
| Infinite iteration                     | Unbounded iterator                   | `itertools.count()`                          | Use `islice` or explicit break.                              |
| Memory blowup                          | Materializing huge generator         | `list(g)`                                    | Stream or batch.                                             |
| `itertools.groupby` groups incorrectly | Data not sorted by same key          | repeated groups                              | Sort first or use dict grouping.                             |

### B.17 Async Errors

| Error/symptom                                                            | Likely cause                            | Example                                        | Fix                                            |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `RuntimeWarning: coroutine was never awaited`                            | Called async function without awaiting  | `fetch()`                                      | `await fetch()` or schedule task.              |
| `SyntaxError: 'await' outside function`                                  | Await outside async context             | script top level                               | Use `asyncio.run(main())`.                     |
| `RuntimeError: no running event loop`                                    | Calling loop-dependent API outside loop | `asyncio.create_task()` in sync code           | Create inside async function or use runner.    |
| `RuntimeError: asyncio.run() cannot be called from a running event loop` | Nested event loop runner                | notebook/framework context                     | Await directly or use framework-provided loop. |
| Event loop stalls                                                        | Blocking call inside async code         | `time.sleep`, sync requests                    | Use async library or executor.                 |
| Tasks disappear/fail silently                                            | Orphan tasks                            | `create_task()` without owner                  | Use `TaskGroup` or store/await task.           |
| Cancellation swallowed                                                   | Broad catch handles cancellation        | `except Exception`/wrong cancellation handling | Clean up and propagate cancellation.           |
| Async resource leak                                                      | Client/session not closed               | HTTP client                                    | Use `async with` or explicit close.            |
| `TypeError: object X can't be used in 'await' expression`                | Awaited non-awaitable                   | `await regular_function()`                     | Remove `await` or make function async.         |

### B.18 Threading and Multiprocessing Errors

| Symptom                        | Likely cause                          | Example                             | Fix                                               |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| Race condition                 | Shared mutable state without lock     | `counter += 1` across threads       | Use lock, queue, or single owner.                 |
| Deadlock                       | Locks acquired in inconsistent order  | thread A waits for B, B waits for A | Define lock order; reduce lock scope.             |
| Threads do not speed CPU work  | Wrong workload model                  | CPU-bound Python loop               | Use process/native/vectorized approach.           |
| Process pool fails to start    | Missing `__main__` guard              | process spawning on import          | Use `if __name__ == "__main__":`.                 |
| Pickling error in process pool | Function/object not serializable      | local function/closure/open file    | Use top-level function and serializable inputs.   |
| Process pool slow              | Tiny tasks or huge payloads           | many small tasks                    | Batch work; reduce transfer.                      |
| Shared DB connection fails     | Resource not process/thread safe      | same connection reused              | Create per-thread/process connection or use pool. |
| Queue blocks forever           | Missing producer/consumer termination | no sentinel                         | Use sentinel, timeout, or task tracking.          |

### B.19 Subprocess Errors

| Error/symptom                            | Likely cause                             | Example                   | Fix                                           |
| ---------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `FileNotFoundError` when running command | Executable not found                     | `subprocess.run(["foo"])` | Check PATH or absolute executable path.       |
| `CalledProcessError`                     | Command exited nonzero with `check=True` | command failed            | Inspect return code/stdout/stderr.            |
| Command hangs                            | Process waits for input or never exits   | no timeout                | Add timeout, provide stdin, or stream output. |
| Shell injection risk                     | Shell string with user input             | `shell=True`              | Pass argument list; avoid shell.              |
| Output is bytes                          | Missing `text=True`                      | `capture_output=True`     | Add `text=True` if text expected.             |
| Huge output memory use                   | Capturing unbounded output               | large stdout              | Stream to file or process incrementally.      |
| Different behavior across platforms      | Shell/path differences                   | Windows vs POSIX          | Avoid shell assumptions; use portable APIs.   |

### B.20 Logging and Diagnostics Errors

| Symptom                            | Likely cause                                                 | Fix                                               |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- |
| No logs appear                     | Logger not configured at app boundary                        | Configure logging in entry point.                 |
| Duplicate logs                     | Multiple handlers or repeated config                         | Configure once; inspect logger hierarchy.         |
| Library changes app logging        | Library calls `basicConfig()`                                | Remove global config from library.                |
| Exception logged without traceback | Used `logger.error` instead of `logger.exception` in handler | Use `logger.exception` inside `except`.           |
| Same exception logged many times   | Logging at every layer                                       | Log at meaningful boundary only.                  |
| Secrets in logs                    | Raw payload/env/token logged                                 | Redact or omit sensitive data.                    |
| Warning not visible                | Warning filters                                              | Configure warning filters or use proper category. |
| Deprecation only logged            | Used logging instead of `warnings.warn`                      | Emit `DeprecationWarning` with `stacklevel`.      |

### B.21 Typing and Static Analysis Diagnostics

| Type checker warning             | Likely cause                                   | Runtime meaning                         | Fix                                           |
| -------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `Optional[...]` has no attribute | Value may be `None`                            | Possible `AttributeError`               | Check `is None` or require value.             |
| Incompatible argument type       | Passed wrong static type                       | May be runtime `TypeError` or logic bug | Fix call or widen/narrow signature correctly. |
| Incompatible return type         | Function returns value not matching annotation | API contract mismatch                   | Fix return or annotation.                     |
| Missing type parameters          | Generic used without parameter                 | Type information lost                   | Use `list[str]`, `dict[str, int]`, etc.       |
| `Any` expression                 | Dynamic value leaked                           | Checker cannot protect downstream code  | Isolate and validate boundary.                |
| Cannot call untyped function     | Untyped dependency/helper                      | Static unknown                          | Add annotations or wrapper.                   |
| `cast` unnecessary               | Checker already knows type                     | Redundant assertion                     | Remove cast.                                  |
| `cast` suspicious                | No runtime proof                               | Type checker silenced only              | Add `isinstance`/validation.                  |
| Protocol mismatch                | Object lacks required method/attribute         | Duck-typed contract not satisfied       | Implement method or change protocol.          |
| TypedDict missing key            | Static dict shape invalid                      | Possible `KeyError`                     | Add required key or mark optional.            |
